title: Ferulago meoides
taxonomy: Ferulago meoides is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It is a member of the genus Ferulago, which is a group of plants that are commonly known as sweet fennels or false fennels. The family Apiaceae, also known as the carrot or parsley family, is a large and diverse group of plants that includes many economically important species. Ferulago meoides is native to the Mediterranean region and is found in dry areas with well-drained soil. This plant is a perennial herb that produces small white or yellow flowers in clusters.

intro: Ferulago meoides, also known as sweet ferulago or wild angelica, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The health benefits of Ferulago meoides include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in traditional medicine for treating various ailments such as digestive issues and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of this plant include ferulic acid, volatile oils, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Ferulago meoides is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and sore throats.
